rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS PANOUTSOPOULOS et al VS. THE KARSANT FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT THE KARSANT FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint, read in the light most favorable to Plaintiff, alleges causation and existence of a qualified buyer/tenant. Plaintiff is not estopped from asserting damages caused by events occurring after April 2013, and demurrer may not be sustained to only a part of a cause of action re: pre April 2013 events. Defendants do not cite any authority to support their position that under the circumstances of this settlement agreement defendant's obligations to negotiate were limited to one time with one prospective buyer. Settlement Agreement was entered into on September 23, 2009 and contemplated submission of "any prospective purchasers" through September 14, 2013. (Settlement Agreement paragraph 6(b)). (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ZHEN HU VS. MARLON AMADOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Marlon Amador'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 15: Defendant Marlon Amador'S Motion For Summary Judgment: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 437c(f)(2). No facts or circumstances newly discovered between July 14, 2014 and July 16, 2014 presented to the Courts. See Defendant's Supplemental declarations in support of the first motion for summary judgment. Even if Defendant sustained its burden under CCP 437c(f)(2), the motion for summary judgment is denied as triable issue of fact remains whether defendant was a holdover subtenant or a new tenancy was created. See Declaration of August De La Cruz filed on July 21, 2014. Request for reasonable expenses is denied as only attorney's fees, which are not recoverable, were requested as sanctions. Collins v. State Department of Transportation (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 859. (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: DOLORES PARK TERRACES, LLC VS. STEPHEN DEMETER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 24, 2014, Line 11: DEFENDANT STEPHEN DEMETER's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges all element of unlawful detainer including expiration of the "3 Day Notice" and relevant rent increases. Defendant does not cite any authority for the proposition that authorized pass-through is not "rent" within the meaning on CCP 1161. Defendant also does not cite any authority for the proposition that understatement of rent owed renders the notice fatally defective. Finally, factual questions such as proper service of the pass though notices and acceptance of rent are not properly raised by demurrer. Defendant and his counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. (501/AJR)
